name: G.I.R.L.S. will be Girls
short_description: A grid based tactical tower defense game set in a fictional Post Apocalyptic world where androids roam the deserted lands.  Inspired by the group ITZY and songs Girls will be Girls / Imaginary Friend.
detailed_description:
GIRLS will be girls

Cute. Tactical. Unforgiving.

GIRLS will be girls is a top-down, 2D grid-based battler where positioning is everything and every turn matters. Command a team of unpredictable girls across tight, turn-based arenas where one bad move can cost the entire run.

Plan carefully. Adapt constantly. Survive the chaos.

Tactical Grid Combat

Every battle plays out on a clean, readable grid.
Movement, spacing, and turn order define survival.

  • Turn-based enemy and player phases

  • Smart positioning and zone control

  • Knockback, freeze, and reactive effects

  • Enemies that close distance and punish mistakes

This isn’t button-mashing — it’s calculated aggression.

Placements That Matter

Placements of players aren’t just numbers — they change how you play.

  • Freeze enemies to delay their turns

  • Control space with crowd effects

  • Manage stacking debuffs and timing windows

  • Turn defensive plays into lethal setups

Momentum swings fast. Control it.

Upgrade & Reward Progression

After each victory, choose carefully.

  • Earn new upgrades mid-run

  • Modify abilities and outcomes

  • Adapt builds based on what you’re offered

  • Push for stretch goals and higher difficulty clears

No two runs feel the same.

Designed for Quick, Strategic Runs

Perfect for:

  • 10–20 minute tactical sessions

  • High-score chasers

  • Players who love “just one more run”

reviews.review_text:
Incredible quality for a solo developer!

The art, sounds, game play, STORY!! is all high quality.

If you like ITZY, turn based games, or tower defense adjacent games you should try it!

a fun indie puzzle/combat game who has a surprising amount of narrative for what I thought would be just a puzzle game.

It's got a compoundingly compelling narrative, good character writing, really unique handpainted art style, and thematically vibey music which kept me around to see how the story unfolded and I started to connect with some of the characters.
Everything feels cohesive and I appreciate the depth the game goes to with connecting gameplay mechanics with the worlds lore.

I really enjoyed throwing on my headphones, going non-verbal, and just immersing myself in the narrative and puzzles. 

Honestly would be willing to pay for this, but since its free there's no reason not to try it out! ^_^
